mission.title: Install and Configure ClickHouse
mission.description: ClickHouse is a real-time analytics database management system designed for generating analytical data reports efficiently. It is particularly useful for applications requiring high-performance data processing and analytics. Key trade-offs include the need for careful schema design and potential complexity in managing distributed setups.

steps.title: Add ClickHouse Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ClickHouse Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install ClickHouse using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add clickhouse https://clickhouse.github.io/clickhouse-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the ClickHouse operator repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the ClickHouse Operator in the specified namespace, which manages ClickHouse clusters.

```bash

kubectl create namespace clickhouse-operator

helm install clickhouse-operator clickhouse/clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse-operator --version 0.24.1

```

> Creates a namespace for the ClickHouse operator.
> Installs the ClickHouse operator with a specific version.

steps.title: Deploy ClickHouse Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a ClickHouse cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD) that the operator manages.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ClickHouse/clickhouse-operator/master/deploy/examples/clickhouse-cluster.yaml

```

> Deploys a sample ClickHouse cluster.

steps.title: Verify ClickHouse Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the ClickHouse cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clickhouseclusters --namespace clickhouse-operator

kubectl logs -l app=clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse-operator

```

> Lists the ClickHouse clusters and their statuses.
> Fetches logs from the ClickHouse operator for troubleshooting.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ClickHouse Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ClickHouse operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if not handled properly.

```bash

helm uninstall clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse-operator

kubectl delete namespace clickhouse-operator

```

> Uninstalls the ClickHouse operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ClickHouse Operator
upgrade.description:
This step involves backing up your data, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get clickhouseclusters --namespace clickhouse-operator -o yaml > backup-clickhouse-cluster.yaml

helm upgrade clickhouse-operator clickhouse/clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse-operator --version 0.25.0

kubectl get clickhouseclusters --namespace clickhouse-operator

```

> Backs up the current ClickHouse cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the ClickHouse operator to the specified version.
> Verifies the status of the ClickHouse clusters after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clickhouseclusters --namespace clickhouse-operator -o yaml > backup-clickhouse-cluster.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create ClickHouse cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure the cluster configuration is correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ClickHouse server is not responding'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the ClickHouse server pod is not running or has crashed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods --namespace clickhouse-operator' to check the pod status and restart if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the ClickHouse service is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service is correctly configured and check network policies.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the ClickHouse pod is being evicted due to memory constraints.

**Fix:**
Increase the memory limits in the ClickHouse cluster configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new SQL functions and improved performance for large datasets.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old configuration format for cluster setup.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v25.x, ensure to migrate your cluster configuration to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the ClickHouse cluster running with the command 'kubectl get clickhouseclusters --namespace clickhouse-operator' showing the status as 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
